page: Amazon Web Services c5d.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8124M processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The instance provides 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, allowing 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 100 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core and memory throughput. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is 1405.09, and the Geekbench single-core score is 1142.0, both falling into the average category. However, multi-core performance is weaker, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3692.85 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 2507.0. Memory bandwidth is also relatively low, with read-only large block speeds at 26.2 GB/sec and write speeds at 23.19 GB/sec. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression at 24.03 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 9.13 GB/sec perform at average levels, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression drops to a poor rating at 16.95 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c5d.xlarge offers dedicated compute resources and high-speed local NVMe storage, making it suitable for workloads requiring low-latency scratch space without the overhead of network-attached storage. However, the low memory-to-vCPU ratio and weaker multi-core scaling represent a tradeoff for memory-intensive or highly parallelized applications. This instance is best suited for single-threaded compute tasks, light web serving, software compilation, and workloads that benefit from fast local temporary storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs from 2 physical Intel Xeon 8124M cores running at 3.4 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a bundled 100 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Benchmark results show average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1142.0 and stress-ng single-core score of 1405.09, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are weaker. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Given its hardware profile, the c5d.xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU cycles and fast local scratch space, such as single-threaded processing, light web serving, and basic software compilation, while presenting tradeoffs in memory-heavy or highly parallelized environments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 100 GB NVMe SSD. It delivers average single-core performance for dedicated workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency scratch space.

8.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 100 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Average single-core performance in CPU and compression benchmarks
Nitro hypervisor architecture with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suitable for single-threaded compute, light web serving, and software compilation
